plan:
-   **Electromagnetics:** SQUID sensors (Superconducting Quantum Interference Devices), magnetic shielding (Mu-metal), lock-in amplifier, low-noise pre-amplifiers, RF filter array. Consider cryocoolers for SQUID operation.
-   **Data Acquisition & Processing:** High-resolution ADC (Analog-to-Digital Converter), FPGA/DSP for real-time signal processing and noise reduction, powerful embedded system (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or Raspberry Pi 4 with custom accelerator) for AI inference.
-   **AI Software:** Develop a neural network model (e.g., CNN or RNN) trained on MCG data and various EMI signatures. Implement real-time adaptive filtering, source separation algorithms, and magnetic field mapping for localization.
-   **Wireless Communication:** High-bandwidth, low-latency wireless module (e.g., WiFi 6E or custom RF link) for data transmission to a control station.
-   **Power Management:** Cryogenic power supply for SQUIDs, high-capacity battery pack for portable operation, power regulation and distribution circuitry for all components.
-   **Mechanical/Structural:** Custom cryostat for SQUID sensors, non-magnetic enclosure (e.g., carbon fiber, aluminum) for electronics, multi-axis robotic arm or gantry system for scanning/localization, vibration isolation platform.

nodes.name: SQUID Sensor Array
nodes.description: Superconducting Quantum Interference Device array for detecting ultra-weak magnetic fields. Requires cryogenic cooling.

nodes.name: Cryocooler
nodes.description: Provides the necessary cryogenic temperatures for SQUID sensor operation without liquid helium.

nodes.name: Low-Noise Pre-Amplifier Array
nodes.description: Amplifies extremely weak SQUID signals with minimal added noise before further processing.
nodes.productName: Analog Devices ADA4807 Quad Pre-Amp Module

nodes.name: RF Filter Array
nodes.description: Passively filters out high-frequency electromagnetic interference before signal amplification.

nodes.name: Multi-Channel Lock-in Amplifier
nodes.description: Extracts small signals from noisy backgrounds using phase-sensitive detection. High-speed multi-channel operation.

nodes.name: High-Resolution ADC Module
nodes.description: Converts analog signals from the lock-in amplifier into high-resolution digital data.

nodes.name: FPGA/DSP Processor
nodes.description: Performs real-time signal conditioning, initial noise reduction, and high-speed data buffering before AI processing.

nodes.name: AI Embedded System
nodes.description: Powerful embedded platform for running AI inference models to filter EMI and localize magnetic fields.

nodes.name: Wireless Communication Module
nodes.description: Provides high-bandwidth, low-latency wireless data transmission to a control station.

nodes.name: SQUID Cryogenic Power Supply
nodes.description: Provides ultra-stable, low-noise current and voltage to the SQUID sensors.

nodes.name: High-Capacity Battery Pack
nodes.description: Primary power source for the entire portable system, offering high capacity and cycle life.

nodes.name: Main Power Distribution Unit
nodes.description: Regulates and distributes power from the battery to various components (e.g., 24V, 12V, 5V, 3.3V rails).

nodes.name: Multi-Axis Motor Driver
nodes.description: Controls multiple stepper motors for the robotic arm or gantry system to scan the SQUID sensors.

nodes.name: Main Base Platform
nodes.description: Heavy-duty, non-magnetic aluminum plate forming the base of the entire system for stability and mounting vibration isolation feet.

nodes.name: Vibration Isolation Feet
nodes.description: Rubber/spring isolation feet to minimize external vibrations transmitted to the system.

nodes.name: Gantry Vertical Extrusions
nodes.description: Sturdy vertical aluminum extrusions to form the uprights of the gantry system.

nodes.name: Gantry X-Axis Extrusion
nodes.description: Horizontal aluminum extrusion for the X-axis movement of the SQUID sensor head.

nodes.name: X-Axis Linear Rail
nodes.description: Precision linear guide rail for smooth and accurate X-axis motion.

nodes.name: X-Axis Linear Carriage
nodes.description: Linear bearing block to move along the X-axis rail.

nodes.name: X-Axis Lead Screw
nodes.description: Lead screw for precise X-axis positioning, driven by a stepper motor.

nodes.name: X-Axis Lead Screw Nut
nodes.description: Anti-backlash nut for accurate and repeatable X-axis motion.

nodes.name: X-Axis Lead Screw Coupler
nodes.description: Connects the X-axis stepper motor to the lead screw.

nodes.name: Main Electronics Enclosure
nodes.description: Non-magnetic aluminum enclosure housing the pre-amplifiers, ADC, FPGA, AI system, and power distribution.

nodes.name: Mu-Metal Shielding Panels
nodes.description: High-permeability alloy panels used to passively shield the SQUID sensors from external magnetic interference.

nodes.name: SQUID Cryostat Outer Housing
nodes.description: Lightweight, non-magnetic carbon fiber housing for the SQUID sensor array and integrated cryocooler.

nodes.name: M5 T-Nuts
nodes.description: Used to connect components to the aluminum extrusions.

nodes.name: M5 Bolts
nodes.description: Fasteners for assembling the gantry structure and mounting components.

nodes.name: M3 PCB Standoffs
nodes.description: Used to mount circuit boards inside the electronics enclosure.

nodes.name: Electronics Board Mounts
nodes.description: Custom mounts designed to securely hold specific PCBs (pre-amp, ADC, FPGA, AI system) within the electronics enclosure.

nodes.name: SQUID Sensor Mount
nodes.description: Internal mount to precisely position the SQUID sensor array within its cryostat housing, non-magnetic.

nodes.name: Cryocooler Mounting Bracket
nodes.description: Custom bracket to securely mount the cryocooler to the main gantry structure or dedicated platform.

nodes.name: Mu-metal Panel Holders
nodes.description: Small clips or frames to hold the Mu-metal shielding panels in place around the SQUID sensor head.

nodes.name: Cable Management Clips
nodes.description: Custom cable clips to route and secure signal and power cables, minimizing movement and potential EMI.

originalPrompt: A sophisticated Device that can measure the Human heart's magnetic field, often measured via magnetocardiography (MCG) with SQUID sensors, is roughly 100 times stronger than the brain's magnetic field, all wirelessly.And also the device should scan the surrounding area and when threre is a Slightest change in the electromagnetic field it can detect that and also it should have a AI software that can filter off all other electromagnetic interference and precisely able to detect the human heart's electromagnetic field and locate the exact position.

subSteps.title: 3D Print all custom components
Prepare Main Base Platform and Gantry Extrusions
Drill and tap mounting holes on Main Base Platform and Electronics Enclosure
Fabricate/Assemble SQUID Cryostat Outer Housing and prepare Mu-Metal Panels
Wire Main Power Distribution Unit and Battery Pack
Connect Pre-Amplifier, RF Filter, Lock-in Amplifier, and ADC modules
Connect FPGA/DSP, AI Embedded System, and Wireless Module
Wire Multi-Axis Motor Driver and Cryocooler power
Connect SQUID Cryogenic Power Supply to SQUID Sensor Array
Perform initial continuity and power rail voltage checks
Install OS, drivers, and necessary libraries on AI Embedded System
Load initial firmware/bitstream onto FPGA/DSP Processor
Verify communication between ADC, FPGA, and AI Embedded System
Test motor driver communication and basic motor control
Configure Wireless Communication Module and verify network connectivity
Initialize SQUID Cryogenic Power Supply and Cryocooler, verify cooling operation
Perform initial testing of SQUID Sensor Array and Lock-in Amplifier (without EMI filtering)
Mount Vibration Isolation Feet to Main Base Platform
Assemble Gantry System (verticals, X-axis extrusion, linear rail, lead screw)
Mount SQUID Cryostat Housing with Cryocooler to Gantry X-Axis Carriage
Integrate SQUID Sensor Array and Mu-Metal Shielding into Cryostat Housing
Mount Electronics Enclosure to Main Base Platform and install internal PCBs
Route all power and data cables using Cable Management Clips
Connect external power supplies (battery pack, cryogenic power supply) and final system checks

projectDescription: A sophisticated Magnetocardiography (MCG) system uses a SQUID sensor array within a cryostat to wirelessly measure the human heart's magnetic field. It employs a multi-axis gantry for precise scanning, while an integrated AI embedded system filters electromagnetic interference and accurately locates the heart's position.

tools: Drill press or hand drill with various bits
Tap and die set (M3, M5, M8)
assumptions: Familiarity with advanced electrical and mechanical assembly techniques
Experience with high-precision motion control systems (gantry/robotics)
Proficiency in embedded Linux for NVIDIA Jetson and FPGA programming (VHDL/Verilog)
Basic understanding of cryogenic systems and SQUID sensor handling
Access to an EMI-controlled environment for sensitive signal testing
Prior experience with setting up and calibrating complex data acquisition systems

mechanicalConnections.label: mounted with M5 bolts and T-nuts
connected with M5 bolts and corner brackets
